comment: Wall-cling / wall-latch (cf. Warframe, Titanfall, Ghostrunner): while airborne and beside a wall, HOLD the `cling` input to LATCH onto the wall — gravity suppressed, position held — for a limited window. INTENT-GATED (you must hold cling beside a wall; it never auto-snaps), so it feels deliberate. PURE DATA: composed onto base.kit.json via kit overlay, no engine code change. It adds ONE new state WALL_CLING and ONE entry transition into AIR (priority 75, between wall-run 70 and wall-jump 85 — never collides). Entry zeroes velocity (set_velocity_y 0 + clamp_speed_h 0) so the body sticks; the tick suppresses gravity to ~0 (apply_gravity scale wall_cling_gravity_scale, with a fall clamp via min_vy) and commits via move_and_slide. A wall_cling timer (stamina/time budget) drains and forces release. EXITS: (1) cling RELEASED → AIR; (2) wall LOST (wall_still_near false) → AIR; (3) timer drained → AIR; (4) JUMP → AIR with the wall-jump grace armed, so the EXISTING base AIR wall-jump transition (input_buffered jump + wall_jump_grace>0, priority 85) launches you off the wall on the same/next tick — wall-jump is reused, not duplicated. All conditions/effects/ordering/tuning are existing primitives; the ONLY composition seam is the new state + transition (data). See docs/decisions/movement-substrate.md §2, §5 (named presets).
comment_vars: §B — wall_cling is a decaying var (the cling time budget): set on latch, drains by dt, forces release via var<=0.
comment_inputs: Declare the `cling` action so the interpreter samples it each tick (input_pressed reads the sampled frame). No buffer/hold — a plain held button.

AIR.comment: Enter WALL_CLING: airborne, a wall is detected on either side, AND the player is HOLDING cling (intent). Priority 75 — above wall-run entry (70) so a held cling latches rather than starts a run, below wall-jump (85)/coyote (90) so a buffered jump still wins. On entry: stick to the wall (zero vertical + horizontal velocity) and arm the cling time budget. wall_detected records wall_normal/wall_side for the tick + wall-jump exit.

transitions.comment: JUMP off the wall: arm the wall-jump grace and hand to AIR. The EXISTING base AIR wall-jump transition (input_buffered jump AND wall_jump_grace>0, priority 85) then fires and launches off wall_normal — we reuse it, no duplicate wall-jump here. Highest priority so a jump always beats the release/timeout exits.
Cling RELEASED → drop off the wall into AIR. Arm coyote so a jump immediately after release still works (parity with leaving any surface).
Wall LOST (no longer near the tracked wall) → AIR.
Cling time budget drained → forced release into AIR.
Landed while clinging (e.g. slid down to the floor) → GROUND.

tick.comment: Suppress gravity (scale ~0) so the body holds on the wall; min_vy clamps any residual fall to a slow controlled creep rather than a hard freeze.
Hold the wall-facing camera roll (deliberate latch feel), signed by the tracked wall side. Render-only.
